title: The Store Where Direction Learned to Say No

features.name: compass shattered
features.desc-on-touch: the cardinal points argue and refuse to recognize each other; you touch it and it redistributes itself into angles that point nowhere

features.name: refusal mirror
features.desc-on-touch: reflection that shows you the way you came, then closes; asks itself not to be seen

features.name: counter saying no
features.desc-on-touch: the register speaks in single syllable refusals; every transaction is declined with courtesy

features.name: floor that exits everywhere
features.desc-on-touch: painted arrows point in every direction except forward; the checkout tile leads back to where you stood before entering

features.name: doorframe refusing entry
features.desc-on-touch: a shop entrance that swings open and closed, open and closed, never quite letting you fully cross the threshold

reverie.title: REVERIE — the shopfront where you both refused to map
narr: you stand at the counter and she stands at the door,
both of you looking at a map that got heavier with each
addition. she says: what if we stop here. what if the
best archive isn't one that grows. you don't argue,
because refusing to grow is the hardest choice of all.

inscription: here nothing points anywhere; refusal is the only direction that holds

file.name: Unmapped_Spaces_Sketches.txt
file.note: pencil sketches of territories intentionally left blank; the archive that chose not to be everywhere

exits.label: toward a shelf that reads itself backward
into a corridor where walls say no to mirrors
